features.description: Implement Agent Skills loader following agentskills.io standard. Skills are directories with SKILL.md containing YAML frontmatter (name, description) plus optional scripts/, references/, assets/ directories. This enables model-driven skill discovery and activation.
expectedBehavior: Discovery: Scan .swell/skills/ and optionally ~/.swell/skills/ for SKILL.md files
Parsing: Extract YAML frontmatter (name, description) from each SKILL.md
Lenient validation: Accept skills with missing optional fields (allow other frontmatter)
Catalog: Build catalog with name, description, location for each skill
Progressive disclosure: Tier 1 (name+description ~50-100 tokens) at startup, Tier 2 (full SKILL.md body) on activation
On-demand resources: Load scripts/, references/, assets/ when explicitly requested
Model-driven activation: LLM reads catalog, matches skills to tasks using description keywords
Skills discoverable by description keywords (e.g., 'rust', 'async', 'tokio' for rust-coding)
verificationSteps: Verify skill catalog includes all skills with name+description
Verify full content loaded only on activation

features.description: Implement Agent Skills loader following agentskills.io standard. Skills are directories with SKILL.md containing YAML frontmatter (name, description) plus optional scripts/, references/, assets/ directories. This enables model-driven skill discovery and activation.
expectedBehavior: Discovery: Scan .swell/skills/ and optionally ~/.swell/skills/ for SKILL.md files
Parsing: Extract YAML frontmatter (name, description) from each SKILL.md
Lenient validation: Accept skills with missing optional fields (allow other frontmatter)
Catalog: Build catalog with name, description, location for each skill
Progressive disclosure: Tier 1 (name+description ~50-100 tokens) at startup, Tier 2 (full SKILL.md body) on activation
On-demand resources: Load scripts/, references/, assets/ when explicitly requested
Model-driven activation: LLM reads catalog, matches skills to tasks using description keywords
Skills discoverable by description keywords (e.g., 'rust', 'async', 'tokio' for rust-coding)
verificationSteps: Verify skill catalog includes all skills with name+description
Verify full content loaded only on activation

features.description: Refactor all SWELL agent prompts following prompt-engineering-expert skill principles. Add XML-wrapped sections, few-shot examples, chain-of-thought requests, and explicit anti-patterns constraints. Also incorporate agentic-coding (contract-first), task-development-workflow (TDD enforcement), and agent-team-orchestration (structured handoffs) principles into prompts.
expectedBehavior: All agent prompts use XML-wrapped sections: <role>, <task>, <context>, <constraints>, <output_format>
Few-shot examples added to PlannerAgent (1-3 plan examples), GeneratorAgent (1-3 code examples), EvaluatorAgent (1-3 evaluation examples)
Chain-of-thought requests in all prompts: 'Think step-by-step before output'
Anti-patterns constraints: 'Do NOT: hallucinate, assume file contents, skip validation, leave TODOs'
PlannerAgent includes problem framing phase with clarification questions when requirements ambiguous
GeneratorAgent enforces TDD: 'write test -> verify fail -> implement -> verify pass -> commit'
Structured handoffs between agents: include 'what done, where artifacts, how verify, known issues, what's next'
Success criteria explicit in each prompt: 'Output must include...'
Context condensation: trigger compaction at 75% token utilization
verificationSteps: Review agents.rs to verify XML sections present
Review agents.rs to verify few-shot examples added
Review agents.rs to verify chain-of-thought requests
Review agents.rs to verify anti-patterns constraints

features.description: Implement multi-run validation following agent-evaluation skill principles. Run validation gates multiple times to detect flakiness, compute statistical confidence, and track behavioral invariants per agent role.
expectedBehavior: EvaluatorAgent runs validation 3x by default for statistical confidence
Behavioral invariants defined per agent role (Planner: plan has steps, Generator: code compiles, Evaluator: returns score)
Track flakiness rate per validation gate
Report statistical confidence in evaluation results

features.description: Implement structured handoffs between agents following agent-team-orchestration skill. Each agent-to-agent handoff includes: what was done, where artifacts are, how to verify, known issues, what's next.
expectedBehavior: GeneratorAgent -> EvaluatorAgent handoff includes structured format
PlannerAgent -> GeneratorAgent handoff includes structured format
Handoff includes 'what done, where artifacts, how verify, known issues, what's next'

features.description: Implement hash-chained audit trail following agent-audit-trail skill. Every tool call, external write, decision, and state transition is logged with sha256 hash chaining for tamper detection. EU AI Act Article 12 compliance.
expectedBehavior: sha256 hash chain linking entries (prev_hash -> hash)
Log tool-call events with actor, target, arguments, result, duration
Log external-write events when agents modify files
Log decision events when agents make significant choices
Log state transitions (task state changes, mode switches)

features.description: OpenTelemetry integration with GenAI semantic conventions (model, tokens, provider, latency, cost attributes).
Event schema: trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, agent_id, session_id, task_id, tool_invocation, timestamp, outcome.
Immutable append-only event log with cryptographic chaining.
expectedBehavior: Previous hash included in each event

features.description: Export traces to Langfuse for analysis.
Trace waterfall view data for task/agent execution.
Correlation IDs linking events across tasks/agents.
Real-time cost tracking per task and run.
Pause/resume, scope modification, instruction injection APIs.
expectedBehavior: APIs accessible via CLI and socket

features.description: Append-only JSONL event log with schema versioning for immutable audit trail.
Immutable timestamped events: tool_invocation, decision, observation, error, outcome stored as episodic memories.
Facts, entities, and relationships stored as graph nodes for semantic knowledge representation.
expectedBehavior: Entities stored as nodes with types
Query by entity or relationship type

features.description: Strategies, procedures, and action patterns stored with Beta posterior distribution for confidence scoring.
Pattern learning from CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files with higher trust weight than agent self-learning.
expectedBehavior: Higher confidence weight than learned patterns

features.description: Self-knowledge: best model per task type, prompting strategies, and learned heuristics for task execution.
expectedBehavior: Tracks model performance by task type

features.description: Alpha/Beta posterior tracking with 0.3-0.6-0.8 threshold zones for confidence level determination.
Time-based decay: procedural memories decay slowly, environmental medium, buffer memories fast.
Pattern promotion: ≥5 confirmations with confidence >0.6. Rule promotion: ≥10 confirmations with confidence >0.8.
expectedBehavior: Promoted patterns get higher retrieval rank

features.description: Mark memories with confidence <0.3 as deprecated with superseded_by link to replacement knowledge.
Mandatory repository scope, optional language and task-type filters for memory operations.
expectedBehavior: Repository scope required for all memory operations

features.description: Reject memories within cosine distance 0.15 of existing memories to prevent duplication.
Invalidate memories that have not been reinforced within configured time windows.
Validate learned procedures against test cases before auto-application in future tasks.
expectedBehavior: Extracted procedures run against golden samples

features.description: Success/failure trajectory analysis to learn from both positive and negative outcomes.
Revert knowledge to prior state by rolling back memory to previous version.
Vector + BM25 + graph traversal with Reciprocal Rank Fusion for hybrid retrieval.
BGE-reranker for cross-encoder reranking of top retrieved candidates.
Detect contradictory memories and resolve using configurable resolution rules.
expectedBehavior: Apply resolution rules (newer wins, higher confidence wins, etc.)

features.description: Link learned facts to source episodes for full traceability of knowledge origin.
expectedBehavior: Track source episode ID per memory
Store evidence and context from source

features.description: LocalExecutor + RemoteExecutor with risk-based routing to appropriate execution environment.
expectedBehavior: Risk classification routes to correct executor

features.description: Firecracker microVM sandbox with <125ms startup time for secure code execution.
gVisor alternative sandbox backend for containerized tool execution.
OS-level sandboxing via Bubblewrap (Linux), Seatbelt (macOS), Landlock (Linux kernel).
Per-worktree environment isolation with separate PATH, env vars, and filesystem view.
External credential proxy so API keys and secrets never enter sandbox environment.
HashiCorp Vault integration for dynamic secrets and credential rotation.
Default-deny outbound network with allowlist for required external services.
Gitleaks and ggshield pre-commit hooks for secret detection in changes.
Auto-redact secret patterns in tool output to prevent credential leakage.
Progressive disclosure for large tool libraries with lazy loading on first access.
Tool metadata: readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint for behavioral classification.
Cedar permission system with formally verifiable policies for tool access control.
Open Policy Agent for infrastructure-level policy enforcement.
Redis-based kill switch outside agent runtime for emergency stop capability.
Resource limits: max turns, wall-clock time, tokens, cost, and failures per session.
Loop detection: same-tool retry, oscillation, and re-planning loop identification.
Auto-run linters and formatters after file edit operations.
Multi-signal validation: tests + lint + types + static analysis + LLM review + spec conformance.
Autonomous CI failure fixing by analyzing failures and proposing corrections.
Extract acceptance criteria from specs and diffs with risk scoring for test selection.
Generate unit, integration, and property-based tests from acceptance criteria.
Stage 0-4 execution: instant, unit, integration, comprehensive with progressive rigor.
Differential coverage analysis, test quarantine, and 3x retry for flaky tests.
5-category failure taxonomy with confidence scoring for test result analysis.
expectedBehavior: Category: implementation bug, test bug, environment issue, flaky, unclear

features.description: Bidirectional links: Goal→Criteria→Tests→Results→Evidence for full traceability.
ML-based test selection per change for intelligent test prioritization (V2).
Mutation testing and static analysis to auto-generate tests for coverage gaps (V2).
Track test value metrics and retire low-value tests that cost more than they provide (V2).
Train model against post-merge defect rates for calibrated validation confidence (V2).
Multi-agent test roles: Planner, Generator, Healer, Reviewer for collaborative testing (V2).
expectedBehavior: Healer agent for fixing broken tests

features.description: L1-Supervised, L2-Guided, L3-Autonomous, L4-FullAuto autonomy levels for task execution.
YAML policy evaluation with deny-first semantics for task and agent operations.
Retry policy: 2 retries same agent, 3rd retry different model, 4+ escalation to human.
Sub-orchestrators for tasks exceeding 15 steps to manage complexity.
Persistent task board with state, assigned agent, time spent, and token cost tracking.
expectedBehavior: Time and cost accounting per task

features.description: Generate follow-up tasks from task completion and acceptance criteria.
Compare specification to codebase to identify missing requirements.
Reject duplicate tasks by similarity scoring and file overlap analysis.
Score tasks 1-5 on spec alignment and blocking impact for prioritization.
Raise approval threshold as run progresses to enforce increasingly strict validation.
Hard limits: max tasks (100), max time (8h), max cost, max failures (10).
Soft limits: warnings at threshold, no-progress detection with configurable timeouts.
60-minute checkpoint interval with progress evaluation for long-running sessions.
Immutable spec reference that cannot be modified once task execution begins.
Stopping conditions: all tasks accepted, stop command received, hard limit breached.
Bounded worker pool with 3-5 workers controlled by semaphore for concurrency management.
Logical file locks to prevent concurrent edits to the same file across agents.
Spawn sub-agents with 2-level maximum depth to prevent runaway agent trees.
Model fallback chain: Sonnet → GPT-4o → Haiku for graceful degradation.
All actions designed to be safe for retry with idempotent semantics.
Backlog aggregation from 4 sources: plan tasks, failure-derived, spec-gap, improvements.
Feature flag infrastructure for instant rollback of problematic changes.
Stacked PRs with small incremental changes under 200 lines per PR.
Risk-tiered merge: auto-merge, auto-merge+AI-review, human-review based on risk level.
GitHub merge queue or Mergify integration for atomic stacked PR merges.
OpenTelemetry with GenAI semantic conventions for model, tokens, latency, cost attributes.
Cryptographic chaining of events for tamper evidence and audit integrity.
Export traces to Langfuse for analysis and observability dashboard.
Hierarchical span structure with tool duration breakdown for trace visualization.
Per-model cost aggregation with detailed breakdown of LLM expenses.
pause_task() and resume_task() APIs for operator intervention in running tasks.
Mid-task instruction injection API for operator guidance during execution.
Task boundary modification API for adding/removing files from task scope.
Full MCP (Model Context Protocol) implementation with tool annotations, outputSchema support, and dynamic tool discovery.
MCP tool metadata annotations for behavioral classification: readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint.
expectedBehavior: All tools have behavioral hint annotations
Automatic risk classification based on hints
Policy engine uses hints for permission decisions

features.description: Tree-sitter AST parsing for semantic code chunking and dependency analysis across 66+ languages.
expectedBehavior: AST parsing for Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, etc.
Dependency extraction from imports and calls

features.description: AST-based dependency graph construction with typed nodes and edges.
expectedBehavior: Nodes: File, Module, Class, Function, Variable, Test
Edges: CALLS, INHERITS_FROM, IMPORTS, DEPENDS_ON, TESTS
Graph query API for dependency analysis

features.description: Property graph knowledge base with typed nodes and edges for code structure representation.
expectedBehavior: Graph database with nodes and edges

features.description: Vector search with code embeddings for semantic memory retrieval.
Full OpenTelemetry integration with GenAI semantic conventions.
Firecracker microVM sandbox with <125ms startup for secure code execution.
Tiered context assembly pipeline: active file, open/recent, vector search, graph-expanded, conversation history.
Integrate with mcp-server-tree-sitter for AST-based code analysis via MCP protocol.
expectedBehavior: SWELL can spawn mcp-server-tree-sitter as subprocess
verificationSteps: Verify tree-sitter server can be started and tools discovered

features.description: Integrate with mcp-language-server + LSP servers (rust-analyzer, clangd) for language intelligence.
expectedBehavior: Bridge MCP to LSP via mcp-language-server
Rust intelligence via rust-analyzer: definitions, references, hover, diagnostics
verificationSteps: Verify LSP tools discoverable and callable

features.description: MCP server configuration system for .swell/mcp_servers.json with server definitions.
expectedBehavior: JSON config file for MCP server definitions
Server name, command, arguments, environment variables
Lazy server startup (only when tools needed)

features.description: Use MCP tree-sitter tools to build dependency graph: functions call X, X imports Y, Y defines Z.
expectedBehavior: Answer queries: 'what depends on X?', 'what does Y call?'

features.description: Use LSP references and tree-sitter analysis to determine impact of proposed changes.
expectedBehavior: Find all usages of a symbol

features.description: Use AST analysis to intelligently chunk context: relevant imports, called functions, called-by functions.
expectedBehavior: Score chunks by relevance to current task
Prioritize called functions and their dependencies
Stay within token budget via intelligent truncation

features.description: Complete TaskStateMachine with all 10 states (Created→Enriched→Ready→Assigned→Executing→Validating→Accepted/Rejected→Failed/Escalated). Invalid transition protection, plan attachment, iteration tracking, escalation after 3 failures.
AgentPool with register/reserve/release/available_count. Thread-safe with proper locking. MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS = 6 enforced.
ExecutionController for Planner→Generator→Evaluator pipeline. execute_task and execute_batch with concurrency limits.
Complete Orchestrator with create_task, get_task, register_agent, assign_task, release_agent, start_task, start_validation, complete_task. Event emission.
Task DAG for dependency tracking. Topological sort for execution ordering. Circular dependency detection.
Policy engine evaluating YAML-defined policies against agent actions. Deny-first evaluation semantics.
Scheduler managing ready queue, task assignment by priority. Configurable max concurrent workers (default: 6, max: 7).
Drift detector comparing modified files against plan. Flags >30% drift from estimated_files.
PlannerAgent with LLM calls to generate structured plans. Reads codebase context, outputs Plan with steps, risk_assessment, estimated_tokens.
GeneratorAgent implementing ReAct loop. Takes Plan steps, uses tools to read/write files, produce code changes.
EvaluatorAgent validating generated code. Runs validation gates, checks sprint contracts, outputs evaluation result with confidence.
CoderAgent for implementing code changes. Narrowly scoped prompts, file editing with diff-based modifications.
TestWriterAgent generating tests from acceptance criteria. Uses repository test patterns as templates.
ReviewerAgent for semantic code review. Checks style, complexity, regressions, conventions.
RefactorerAgent for code restructuring. Preserves behavior while improving structure.
DocWriterAgent for documentation generation. Generates/modifies docs from code changes.
SystemPromptBuilder assembling agent context from project config, conventions, memory blocks, task context.
ReAct loop implementation (Think→Act→Observe→Repeat) for agent tool use with reflection on failures.
Context condensation at 75% window utilization. Auto-compact to prevent overflow.
Fix CoderAgent: Integrate LLM backend in execute() to generate actual code instead of using format string fallback. The llm field exists but is not used.
expectedBehavior: CoderAgent.execute() uses LLM backend for code generation
Falls back to heuristic only when LLM unavailable

features.description: Fix RefactorerAgent: Implement post-refactor validation to verify behavior is preserved after refactoring. Current implementation identifies opportunities but never validates after refactoring.

Fix RefactorerAgent: Wire apply_refactor() into execute(), run validation before AND after refactoring, integrate revert logic when validation fails. Current issues: (1) execute() never calls apply_refactor(), (2) before_passed hardcoded to true, (3) reverted hardcoded to false.
expectedBehavior: before_passed captures actual test result before refactor
reverted set correctly when validation fails after refactor

features.description: Fix RefactorerAgent: Call apply_refactor() in execute() for refactoring opportunities. Currently apply_refactor() method exists but is never invoked - it's dead code. Must actually invoke the method to apply refactored code.
expectedBehavior: execute() calls apply_refactor() for identified opportunities
Actual code changes are generated and applied
No more simulated-only refactoring with comments

features.description: Fix daemon socket reading bug in server.rs: vec![0u8; 4096] pre-initializes buffer causing tokio read_buf() to read 0 bytes. Fix: change to Vec::with_capacity(4096). This blocks CLI and daemon assertions.
expectedBehavior: Socket reads actual data instead of 0 bytes

features.description: Fix CLI client socket reading bug in swell-cli/src/main.rs: vec![0u8; 65536] pre-initializes buffer causing read_buf() to return 0 bytes. Same fix as daemon - change to Vec::with_capacity(65536). Blocks VAL-CLI-001 through VAL-CLI-004.
expectedBehavior: CLI reads actual data from socket

features.description: FileReadTool reading file contents with path validation. Respects workspace boundaries.
FileWriteTool creating/overwriting files. Atomic writes with rollback on failure.
FileEditTool for diff-based modifications. Shows diffs before/after, requires confirmation in pair mode.
ShellTool executing commands. Captures stdout/stderr, returns exit code. Respects timeout.
GitTool with git_status, git_diff, git_log, git_commit. Branch naming: agent/<task-id>/<description>.
SearchTool with grep, glob, symbol_search. Codebase navigation and pattern matching.
ToolRegistry with register/get/list. MCP-compatible schemas, tool discovery.
MCP client for external tool servers. JSON-RPC over stdio, tool discovery, deferred loading.
Permission tier system: Auto (approve), Ask (confirm), Deny (block). Risk classification per tool.
AnthropicBackend with full chat() implementation. Tool support, streaming, error handling.
OpenAIBackend with full chat() implementation for GPT models.
MockLlm for testing. Predictable responses, configurable, failure simulation.
Model router selecting appropriate model per task type. Sonnet for coding, Opus for planning, Haiku for fast tasks.
LLM health check system. Per-backend health monitoring, fallback on failure.

features.description: LintGate running clippy/rustfmt. Parses output for errors/warnings, returns ValidationOutcome.
TestGate running cargo test. Maps failures to implementation/test/environment defects.
SecurityGate running Semgrep/CodeQL. SAST scanning, vulnerability detection.
AiReviewGate with Evaluator agent for AI-powered code review. Confidence scoring.
ValidationPipeline running gates in order. Aggregates results, overall pass requires all pass.

features.description: Confidence scoring from multiple signals. Coverage, mutation score, static analysis.
Flakiness detection using historical patterns. DeFlaker approach, quarantine pool.
Evidence pack generation with test results, coverage, validation signals for PR review.
SqliteCheckpointStore with save/load/list/load_latest. Task state persistence across restarts.
InMemoryCheckpointStore for tests. No database required.
PostgresCheckpointStore for production. Event-sourced schema, append-only events.
CheckpointManager persisting at every state transition. Agent checkpointing after each tool call.
Fix state-checkpoint-manager: transition_with_checkpoint must call is_valid_transition before performing state transition. Currently allows invalid transitions to be checkpointed.
Fix state-checkpoint-manager: Integrate CheckpointManager into orchestrator for agent checkpointing after each tool call. Currently CheckpointManager is not connected to the execution loop.

features.description: Fix orchestrator-checkpoint integration: GeneratorAgent must receive orchestrator's checkpoint manager. Orchestrator has checkpoint_manager field and with_checkpoint_manager() method, but they are never connected. Wire in execute_task flow so GeneratorAgent receives checkpoint manager and checkpoints after each tool call.
